name: 弗朗茨·李斯特
brief: 李斯特出生于匈牙利的莱丁（今奥地利境内），被公认为音乐史上最伟大的钢琴演奏家。他是第一位举办现代意义上的独奏音乐会（Recital）的钢琴家，所到之处引发近乎疯狂的「李斯特狂热」（Lisztomania）。中年后他退出舞台，定居魏玛专注于作曲与指挥，晚年接受低品神职成为修道院院长。他的慷慨无私在音乐界传为佳话——他无偿帮助了无数年轻音乐家，包括瓦格纳。
briefEn: Liszt was born in Raiding, Hungary (now in Austria), and is widely regarded as the greatest pianist in the history of music. He was the first to give solo piano recitals in the modern sense, sparking near-hysterical 'Lisztomania' wherever he performed. In middle age he retired from the concert stage and settled in Weimar to focus on composing and conducting; in his later years he took minor holy orders and became an abbe. His legendary generosity toward fellow musicians — he aided countless young artists, including Wagner, free of charge — remains celebrated to this day.
contribution: 李斯特发明了交响诗（Symphonic Poem）这一全新体裁，将文学、绘画与哲学的内容注入管弦乐。他开创了「主题变形」（Thematic Transformation）技法——一个主题在作品中不断变形以表达不同情感和场景。他是最早在音乐中系统使用匈牙利音阶和吉普赛元素的作曲家，对民族乐派产生了深远影响。
contributionEn: Liszt invented the symphonic poem, an entirely new genre that infused orchestral music with literary, visual, and philosophical content. He pioneered the technique of thematic transformation — reshaping a single motif throughout a work to express different emotions and scenes. He was among the first composers to systematically employ Hungarian scales and Roma musical elements, profoundly influencing the nationalist schools that followed.
style: 李斯特的音乐风格跨度极大。早中期作品以炫目的钢琴技巧和华丽的管弦乐效果著称——八度狂飙、震音轰鸣、大幅度跳跃，将钢琴的物理极限推到了前所未有的高度。然而晚期作品（如《灰色的云》《悲伤的贡多拉》）则走向了另一个极端——极度简约、和声模糊、充满空白与沉默，预示了德彪西甚至20世纪的实验音乐。
styleEn: Liszt's stylistic range is enormous. His early and middle-period works dazzle with virtuosic pianism and orchestral brilliance — thundering octaves, roaring tremolos, and sweeping leaps that pushed the physical limits of the piano to unprecedented extremes. His late works, however, such as Nuages gris and La lugubre gondola, move to the opposite pole — radically spare, harmonically ambiguous, filled with silence and empty space, foreshadowing Debussy and even the experimental music of the twentieth century.

famousScores.name: 《b小调奏鸣曲》 S.178
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Sonata in B minor, S. 178
famousScores.desc: 单乐章奏鸣曲，浪漫主义钢琴文献中最宏伟的建筑
famousScores.descEn: A single-movement sonata and the grandest architectural achievement in the Romantic piano repertoire
famousScores.detail:
《b小调奏鸣曲》完成于1853年，是李斯特献给罗伯特·舒曼的作品（舒曼回赠以《C大调幻想曲》，但两人的友好交流后来因风格分歧而冷却）。这部奏鸣曲以极端的方式打破了传统奏鸣曲的多乐章结构——整部作品由一个单一的庞大乐章构成，长达30余分钟，但内部包含了类似于奏鸣曲式、慢乐章和终曲的多个段落，以「主题变形」技法将三个核心动机贯穿始终。

首演于1857年由汉斯·冯·彪罗在柏林担任钢琴独奏。演出结束后，坐在台下的勃拉姆斯和克拉拉·舒曼据说在终曲的巨大高潮前睡着了（这一故事被李斯特的支持者视为对保守派的最好嘲讽）。然而这部作品在历史上逐渐赢得了无可争议的地位，20世纪的钢琴家们（霍洛维茨、波利尼、阿格里奇）纷纷将其列为整个钢琴文献的至高峰之一。

《b小调奏鸣曲》从音乐史的角度看，是浪漫主义钢琴音乐在形式实验方面迈出的最大一步。它以单乐章结构整合奏鸣原则的做法直接影响了理查·施特劳斯的交响诗和勋伯格的《室内交响曲》，证明大型音乐形式不必依赖多乐章的外在框架，而可以依靠内在逻辑实现统一。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Sonata in B minor was completed in 1853 and dedicated to Robert Schumann (who responded with his Fantasie in C major, though the two men's friendly exchange later cooled over stylistic differences). The sonata shatters the conventional multi-movement structure in the most radical way: the entire work is a single enormous movement lasting over thirty minutes, yet internally it contains passages analogous to a sonata-form exposition, a slow movement, and a finale, with three core motifs threaded throughout by means of thematic transformation.

The premiere was given by Hans von Bülow in Berlin in 1857. The story goes that Brahms and Clara Schumann, seated in the audience, fell asleep before the work's climactic close — a tale long relished by Liszt's supporters as the perfect rebuke to the conservative camp. Historically, however, the sonata has achieved unquestioned stature, and twentieth-century pianists from Horowitz to Pollini to Argerich have ranked it among the supreme peaks of the entire piano literature.

From a historical perspective, the B minor Sonata represents the boldest formal experiment in Romantic piano music. Its integration of sonata principles within a single-movement structure directly influenced Richard Strauss's symphonic poems and Schoenberg's Chamber Symphony, proving that large-scale musical architecture need not depend on the external frame of multiple movements but can achieve unity through internal logic alone.
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Liszt: Piano Sonata in B Minor, S. 178

famousScores.name: 《超技练习曲》 S.139
famousScores.desc: 12首练习曲，钢琴技巧的珠穆朗玛峰
famousScores.descEn: Twelve etudes representing the Everest of piano technique
famousScores.detail:
《超技练习曲》经历了三次版本的演变：最初的雏形来自李斯特1826年的少年习作，1837年他修订为技术要求极度苛刻的版本（据说当时世界上只有他自己能够演奏），最终1851年定稿为现在通行的版本。12首练习曲每首都有一个诗意标题（如「鬼火」「马戏团」「英雄」），将练习曲提升为充满标题性想象力的音乐诗篇，是对肖邦练习曲精神的延伸，但在技术难度上远远超越。

其中「鬼火」（Feux follets）一曲被许多钢琴家认为是整个键盘文献中技术最困难的作品之一：双手同时演奏极度轻盈的半音颤音，如幽灵火光在黑暗中忽闪忽灭，稍有不慎便会变成一片混乱。「英雄」（Eroica）则是宏大壮阔的英雄史诗，充满李斯特式的激情与气魄。李斯特本人1837年演奏初版时，据说令在场的肖邦和帕格尼尼都目瞪口呆。

《超技练习曲》在钢琴教学史上奠定了现代钢琴技术的最高标准。它对20世纪钢琴家的体能和心理素质提出了全新要求，直接影响了拉赫玛尼诺夫、斯克里亚宾的练习曲写作，以及20世纪末以戈多夫斯基为代表的「超越肖邦-李斯特传统」的极端技术探索。直到今天，能够令人信服地演奏全套《超技练习曲》的钢琴家屈指可数。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Transcendental Etudes went through three phases of revision: an initial version sketched by the teenage Liszt in 1826; a ferociously demanding 1837 revision said to be playable by no one alive but Liszt himself; and the final 1851 version in use today. All twelve carry poetic titles (Feux follets, Mazeppa, Eroica, and others), elevating the etude to a genre of pictorial musical imagination — an extension of Chopin's spirit, but surpassing it in technical extremity.

Feux follets ('Will-o'-the-Wisps') is considered by many pianists the most technically difficult piece in the entire keyboard literature: both hands simultaneously execute extremely delicate chromatic trills, like ghostly flames flickering in darkness; the slightest lapse produces chaos. Eroica is a grand heroic epic full of Lisztian passion and sweep. When Liszt performed the 1837 version, Chopin and Paganini were reportedly both left speechless.

The Transcendental Etudes established the supreme technical standards of modern pianism. They set new demands on the physical and psychological resources of twentieth-century pianists, directly influencing the etude writing of Rachmaninoff and Scriabin and the extreme technical explorations of Godowsky and others who sought to 'surpass' the Chopin-Liszt tradition. To this day, the number of pianists who can play the complete set convincingly can be counted on one hand.
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Transcendental Etudes, S. 139: No. 10 in F Minor

famousScores.name: 《匈牙利狂想曲》 S.244
famousScores.desc: 19首，尤以第2号最为著名，汤姆和杰瑞的经典配乐
famousScores.descEn: Nineteen rhapsodies — No. 2 is the most famous, immortalized in the Tom and Jerry cartoons
famousScores.detail:
《匈牙利狂想曲》的19首作品创作于1851至1885年间，是李斯特对匈牙利-罗姆人（吉普赛）民间音乐的系统整理与艺术升华。每首狂想曲通常以缓慢忧郁的「拉苏」（Lassan）段落开始，然后转入快速热烈的「弗里斯卡」（Friss/Friska）段落，这种结构源自匈牙利维尔班科斯（Verbunkos）军乐传统。李斯特将这些民间素材融入了最炫目的钢琴技法之中。

第2号（升c小调）是整套作品中最广为人知的一首，它在美国动画《汤姆和杰瑞》系列中多次作为高速追逐场面的配乐，从而获得了全球性的知名度。据说李斯特本人也喜欢在音乐会安可时演奏这首曲子——他的弟子Carl Tausig还为这首作品增加了更多的炫技装饰，形成了今天最常用的「Tausig版本」。

《匈牙利狂想曲》在音乐史上是第一批真正意义上将非欧洲中心的民族音乐素材（匈牙利和罗姆人音乐）提升为严肃艺术形式的钢琴作品之一。它直接启发了勃拉姆斯的《匈牙利舞曲》、德沃夏克的斯拉夫舞曲，以及整个19世纪民族乐派运动。巴托克后来对李斯特对民间音乐的处理方式提出了批评（认为不够「真实」），但这正说明了李斯特对这一议题的开创性贡献。
famousScores.detailEn:
The nineteen Hungarian Rhapsodies, composed between 1851 and 1885, represent Liszt's systematic collection and artistic transformation of Hungarian-Roma folk music. Each rhapsody typically opens with a slow, melancholy Lassan section before moving to a fast and fiery Friss (or Friska), a structure derived from the Hungarian Verbunkos military music tradition. Liszt wove these folk materials into the most dazzling pianism imaginable.

No. 2 in C-sharp minor is the most universally recognized of the set, achieving global fame as the soundtrack to multiple high-speed chase scenes in the Tom and Jerry cartoon series. Liszt himself reportedly enjoyed performing it as an encore; his pupil Carl Tausig added additional virtuosic ornaments, producing the 'Tausig version' most commonly heard today.

Historically, the Hungarian Rhapsodies were among the first piano works to elevate non-Western-European folk materials — specifically Hungarian and Roma music — into serious art forms. They directly inspired Brahms's Hungarian Dances, Dvořák's Slavonic Dances, and the entire nineteenth-century nationalist movement. Bartók later criticized Liszt's handling of folk materials as insufficiently 'authentic,' but this critique itself testifies to the pioneer importance of Liszt's contribution to the field.
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Hungarian Rhapsody No. 2 in C-Sharp Minor, S. 244/2

famousScores.name: 《前奏曲》 S.97
famousScores.desc: 最著名的交响诗，人生如一系列前奏曲
famousScores.descEn: His most celebrated symphonic poem: life as a series of preludes
famousScores.detail:
《前奏曲》是李斯特13首交响诗中最广为人知的一首，首演于1854年魏玛。作品以法国诗人拉马丁的《诗的沉思》中的一句话为题铭：「我们的生命，不过是死亡所奏出的那首歌的前奏曲。」李斯特以此为哲学出发点，将整部交响诗分为爱情、暴风雨、田园宁静与战斗凯旋四个段落，以「主题变形」技法将一个核心动机贯穿始终。

《前奏曲》在20世纪经历了一段令人不安的历史：它的胜利主题段落被纳粹德国的广播电台选为「东线战报」（OKW战报）的开场音乐，每当德军宣布胜利就会奏响这段音乐。这个历史事实导致这部作品在战后的德国演出界一度非常敏感——尽管李斯特本人当然与纳粹毫无关系，但音乐与历史语境之间的纠缠关系给这部作品留下了复杂的阴影。

从音乐史的角度看，《前奏曲》是交响诗这一体裁最完整、最成功的展示之一。它证明了管弦乐可以不依赖叙事情节，仅凭哲学主题和「主题变形」技法就能构建一部完整而有说服力的音乐叙事。理查·施特劳斯的《堂吉诃德》《英雄的生涯》和圣-桑斯的交响诗系列都直接继承了《前奏曲》的体裁遗产。
famousScores.detailEn:
Les Préludes is the most celebrated of Liszt's thirteen symphonic poems, premiered in Weimar in 1854. The work takes as its epigraph a line from the French poet Lamartine's Méditations poétiques: 'What is our life but a series of preludes to that unknown hymn whose first solemn note is sounded by Death?' From this philosophical premise, Liszt structured the work in four sections — love, storm, pastoral calm, and martial triumph — threading a single core motif throughout by means of thematic transformation.

The work acquired a disturbing historical association in the twentieth century: its triumphant theme was chosen by Nazi Germany's radio as the fanfare for the OKW (High Command) war bulletins on the Eastern Front, broadcast whenever German victories were announced. This fact made performances of the work awkward in postwar Germany for some time — though Liszt himself obviously had no connection to Nazism — leaving a complex shadow over the work's reception.

Historically, Les Préludes is one of the most complete and successful demonstrations of the symphonic poem genre. It proved that an orchestra, without recourse to narrative story, could construct a fully convincing musical argument from philosophical theme and thematic transformation alone. Richard Strauss's Don Quixote and Ein Heldenleben, and Saint-Saëns's symphonic poems, all directly inherit the genre legacy of Les Préludes.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Franz Liszt, Berliner Philharmoniker, Herbert von Karajan
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Liszt: Orchestral Works - Compilation by Franz Liszt
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Les préludes, Symphonic Poem No. 3, S. 97

famousScores.name: 《爱之梦》第三首 S.541
famousScores.desc: 「只要你能爱」，可能是李斯特最优美的旋律
famousScores.descEn: 'O Love, as Long as You Can' — perhaps Liszt's most beautiful melody
famousScores.detail:
《爱之梦》三首钢琴曲是李斯特自己的三首艺术歌曲的钢琴改编版。第三首（降A大调）改编自他的歌曲《只要你能爱》（O lieb, so lang du lieben kannst），歌词来自德国诗人弗赖利格拉特（Ferdinand Freiligrath）。原诗是对爱情珍惜的劝诫：趁还能爱的时候去爱，因为总有一天你将痛失所爱，那时候悔之晚矣。

这首曲子的左手分解和弦伴奏型是李斯特的独创——大跨度的分解和弦要求演奏者的左手在键盘上进行宽广的跳跃，同时保持流畅柔和的音响效果，技术难度超出了外表的简约印象。中段的转调（转向E大调的短暂热情高潮）是整首作品中最令人心旷神怡的时刻，仿佛一阵突然迸发的强烈情感。李斯特在写给友人的信中曾说这首曲子是「送给所有爱着的人的礼物」。

《爱之梦》第三首是钢琴音乐会曲目中最受大众欢迎的作品之一，它以极度可亲的旋律美感弥合了「严肃古典音乐」与普通听众之间的隔阂。在通俗文化中，这首曲子被用于无数浪漫场景的背景音乐，也是无数钢琴学生的必学曲目。它证明了李斯特并非只会写高难度的炫技曲，他同样能写出打动最普通听众的温柔旋律。
famousScores.detailEn:
The three Liebesträume ('Dreams of Love') are piano transcriptions of three of Liszt's own art songs. The third, in A-flat major, is a transcription of 'O lieb, so lang du lieben kannst' ('O Love, as Long as You Can Love'), setting a poem by Ferdinand Freiligrath that urges cherishing love while it lasts — for the time will come when those you love are gone, and regret will be too late.

The left-hand broken-chord accompaniment pattern is a Liszt invention: wide-spread arpeggios require the left hand to make sweeping leaps across the keyboard while maintaining a smooth, gentle sonority — technically more demanding than the outward simplicity suggests. The modulation in the middle section (a brief passionate climax in E major) is the most exhilarating moment in the piece, like a sudden surge of intense feeling. Liszt wrote to a friend that this piece was 'a gift to all who love.'

Liebestraum No. 3 is among the most beloved pieces in the piano recital repertoire, bridging the distance between 'serious classical music' and general audiences through its irresistibly singable melody. In popular culture it serves as background music for countless romantic scenes and is an essential piece in the repertoire of piano students everywhere. It proves that Liszt was not exclusively a composer of high-difficulty showpieces — he could also write the most touching and accessible of melodies.
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Liszt - Wild and Crazy - Compilation by Franz Liszt
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Liebestraum No.3 In A-Flat Major, S.541 No.3: Notturno III: O lieb, so lang du lieben kannst

famousScores.name: 《但丁奏鸣曲》（读但丁之后）
famousScores.nameEn: Après une lecture du Dante: Fantasia quasi Sonata
famousScores.desc: 《巡礼之年》第二集中的钢琴宏篇，以音乐重现但丁《神曲》的地狱与天堂
famousScores.descEn: The monumental centrepiece of Années de pèlerinage Year II — a musical journey through Dante's Inferno and Paradise
famousScores.detail:
《但丁奏鸣曲》（正式标题为《读但丁之后：幻想奏鸣曲》）是《巡礼之年·第二年·意大利》（S.161）中的压轴曲，创作于1837至1849年间，灵感来自维克多·雨果的同名诗作以及但丁的《神曲》。全曲以一个令人不寒而栗的减七和弦开场，以及从低音区骤然跃入高音区的「地狱倒影」手法，描绘地狱的混沌与恐惧；中间的慢段以一首近乎天国般纯净的旋律描绘天堂的安宁，与地狱段落形成极端对比。

这部作品在技术上极为艰险——地狱段落中的双手交叉、连续滑奏和八度狂飙将当时钢琴技术推到了极限。李斯特将手稿交给肖邦审阅，肖邦据说委婉地建议他「稍微简化一下」，李斯特礼貌地拒绝了这个建议，并说：「我想保留它原来的样子，无法修改。」这段佳话揭示了两位钢琴巨匠在音乐美学上的根本分歧——肖邦追求内在歌唱，李斯特追求外在宏壮。

《但丁奏鸣曲》在李斯特的钢琴作品中具有里程碑意义，它是《b小调奏鸣曲》之前最接近「宏大形式」的钢琴作品，也是浪漫主义时代文学-音乐互文关系最深刻的范本之一。它影响了巴托克的《蓝胡子公爵的城堡》开场以及许多20世纪键盘作品中以减七和弦象征恐怖与深渊的写法。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Dante Sonata (full title: Après une lecture du Dante: Fantasia quasi Sonata) is the culminating work of Années de pèlerinage: Deuxième Année, Italie (S. 161), composed between 1837 and 1849, inspired by Victor Hugo's poem of the same title and by Dante's Divine Comedy. The work opens with a chilling diminished-seventh chord and a sudden leap from the bass register to the treble — the 'Infernal mirror' — evoking the chaos and terror of Hell; a central Andante section offers a nearly celestial melody of pure serenity, contrasting absolutely with the demonic outer passages.

The technical demands are fearsome — the hellish sections' hand-crossings, sweeping glissandos, and thundering octaves pushed the limits of pianism at the time. Liszt showed the manuscript to Chopin, who reportedly gently suggested 'simplifying it somewhat.' Liszt politely declined: 'I want it to stay as it is; I cannot change it.' This exchange captures the fundamental aesthetic divide between the two piano giants — Chopin seeking inner song, Liszt pursuing outer grandeur.

The Dante Sonata stands as a landmark in Liszt's piano output, the closest thing to a 'grand form' he wrote before the B minor Sonata, and one of the most profound examples of literary-musical intertextuality in the Romantic era. Its influence can be heard in the opening of Bartók's Duke Bluebeard's Castle and in the use of diminished-seventh chords to signify terror and the abyss in many twentieth-century keyboard works.
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Liszt: Années de pèlerinage, Deuxième Année, Italie, S. 161: Après une lecture du Dante (Fantasia quasi Sonata)

famousScores.name: 《梅菲斯托圆舞曲》第一首 S.514
famousScores.desc: 以浮士德传说为灵感的魔鬼圆舞曲，狂野而不羁，钢琴炫技的顶峰之一
famousScores.descEn: A diabolical waltz inspired by the Faust legend — wild, demonic, and among the supreme tests of piano virtuosity
famousScores.detail:
《梅菲斯托圆舞曲》第一首（S.514，也被称为「乡村酒馆的舞蹈」）创作于1859至1861年间，改编自尼古拉斯·莱瑙（Nikolaus Lenau）的戏剧长诗《浮士德》中的一个场景：梅菲斯托（魔鬼）抢过一位村民的提琴，在乡村酒馆里拉起疯狂的舞曲，诱惑浮士德和一位美丽的女孩在月光下陷入爱欲的旋涡。这首作品原为管弦乐版本（作为《浮士德交响诗》的插曲），后来李斯特自己改编为钢琴独奏版，即现在广为人知的形式。

这首圆舞曲以一个令人不安的开场著称：两只手在空弦（A弦和E弦的纯五度）上反复拨奏，仿佛梅菲斯托正在调音，一种莫名的邪气从寂静中悄悄聚集。之后音乐逐渐热烈，发展至令人窒息的高潮——连续的八度狂飙、飞速的三度颤音、大幅度的跳跃，将钢琴技术推向极限。中间一段迷幻的慢板（爱情场景）与外部的疯狂形成鲜明对比，营造出一种真正令人不安的美丽。

《梅菲斯托圆舞曲》第一首是李斯特晚期风格过渡的重要节点：它已不再是早期那种纯粹炫技的华丽，而是将炫技与深刻的戏剧叙事完美融合，预示了他后期作品中越来越明显的戏剧性简约。这首作品对拉赫玛尼诺夫的《音画练习曲》、斯克里亚宾的晚期奏鸣曲和巴托克的恶魔式钢琴写作均有直接影响，是理解李斯特如何将通俗的舞曲形式提升为深刻艺术表达的最佳范例。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Mephisto Waltz No. 1 (S. 514, also called 'The Dance in the Village Inn') was composed in 1859–61, adapted from a scene in Nikolaus Lenau's dramatic poem Faust: Mephisto (the devil) seizes a peasant's fiddle and strikes up a wild dance in a village tavern, seducing Faust and a beautiful girl into a moonlit whirlpool of erotic passion. It was originally an orchestral piece (an episode in a Faust Symphonic Poem) before Liszt transcribed it for solo piano in the form now universally known.

The waltz opens with a distinctly unsettling gesture: both hands repeatedly pluck open strings (a bare perfect fifth on A and E), as if Mephisto is tuning his instrument, and a nameless evil energy slowly coalesces from the silence. The music then builds to a breathless climax — thundering octaves, flying thirds, sweeping leaps — pushing piano technique to its limits. A dreamy central Andante (the love scene) contrasts sharply with the surrounding frenzy, creating a genuinely disturbing beauty.

The Mephisto Waltz No. 1 stands at an important transitional point toward Liszt's late style: it is no longer merely brilliant for brilliance's sake, but perfectly fuses virtuosity with profound dramatic narrative — foreshadowing the increasingly stark dramatic economy of his later works. This piece directly influenced Rachmaninoff's Études-tableaux, Scriabin's late sonatas, and Bartók's demonic piano writing, and it stands as the finest example of how Liszt elevated the popular dance form into serious artistic expression.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Franz Liszt, Berliner Philharmoniker, Herbert von Karajan
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Herbert von Karajan - Invitation to the Dance - Compilation by Herbert von Karajan

famousScores.name: 《巡礼之年》第一年（瑞士）
famousScores.nameEn: Années de pèlerinage, Year I: Suisse, S. 160
famousScores.desc: 李斯特与玛丽·达古尔伯爵夫人私奔瑞士时所作，以音乐描绘阿尔卑斯山的壮阔与内心的震撼
famousScores.descEn: Written during Liszt's romantic exile in Switzerland with Countess Marie d'Agoult — musical paintings of the Alps and the soul
famousScores.detail:
《巡礼之年·第一年·瑞士》是李斯特将文学、绘画与音乐融为一体的最早尝试之一，创作于1835至1855年间。1835年，李斯特与有夫之妇玛丽·达古尔伯爵夫人私奔至瑞士日内瓦，两人在那里度过了一段隐居的激情岁月——他们的两个女儿（包括后来嫁给瓦格纳的科西玛）就是在此期间出生的。这套作品中的每首曲子都以自然景观或文学作品为题，是这段私奔生涯的音乐日记。

组曲的高峰是第六首《瓦伦斯塔特湖畔》（Au lac de Wallenstadt）和第七首《泉水旁》（Au bord d'une source）——前者以轻柔的波纹模仿湖水的荡漾，后者以水晶般清澈的分解和弦描绘山间泉流，都是印象主义钢琴音乐最直接的预言之作。最后一首《暴风雨》（Orage）以排山倒海的力度和速度描绘阿尔卑斯山的雷雨，将钢琴的物理表现力推向了自然元素描绘的极限。

《巡礼之年》三册是李斯特最具文化深度的钢琴作品，充分展示了他「文学钢琴家」的面向。第一年（瑞士）直接影响了德彪西的《前奏曲》（尤其是水声描绘方面）和拉威尔的《水精》，奠定了19世纪晚期至20世纪初印象主义钢琴写作中「自然风景描绘」传统的起点。
famousScores.detailEn:
Années de pèlerinage, Year I: Suisse is one of Liszt's earliest attempts to fuse literature, painting, and music, composed between 1835 and 1855. In 1835 Liszt eloped to Geneva with the married Countess Marie d'Agoult; they spent a passionate period of seclusion in Switzerland, where their two daughters — including Cosima, later Wagner's wife — were born. Each piece in the set takes a landscape or literary work as its subject, forming a musical diary of this romantic exile.

The suite's highlights include the sixth piece, Au lac de Wallenstadt, whose gentle rippling figures imitate the lapping of a Swiss lake, and Au bord d'une source, whose crystal-clear broken chords depict a mountain spring — both are among the most direct premonitions of Impressionist piano writing ever penned. The final piece, Orage ('Storm'), depicts an Alpine thunderstorm with overwhelming force and velocity, pushing the piano's physical capacities for depicting elemental nature to their limits.

The three volumes of Années de pèlerinage are Liszt's most culturally rich piano works, fully displaying his identity as a 'literary pianist.' Year I directly influenced Debussy's Preludes (especially in aquatic imagery) and Ravel's Ondine, establishing the starting point of the 'landscape depiction' tradition in late nineteenth- and early twentieth-century Impressionist piano writing.
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: The Romantic Piano I - Compilation by Daniel Barenboim
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Années de pèlerinage I, Suisse, S. 160: I. Chapelle de Guillaume Tell

famousScores.name: 《帕格尼尼大练习曲》第三首「钟」 S.141
famousScores.nameEn: Grandes Études de Paganini No. 3 'La Campanella,' S. 141
famousScores.desc: 改编自帕格尼尼小提琴协奏曲的钢琴炫技奇迹，铃声般的清澈与速度令人叹为观止
famousScores.descEn: Transcribed from Paganini's Violin Concerto — bell-like clarity and dazzling velocity that leave audiences breathless
famousScores.detail:
《帕格尼尼大练习曲》第三首「钟」（La Campanella）创作于1851年，是李斯特对帕格尼尼《b小调第二小提琴协奏曲》（Op.7）末乐章的钢琴改编版。「钟」这个标题来自帕格尼尼原作中模仿小铃铛音色的独奏小提琴技法——李斯特将这个效果移植到钢琴上，以高音区的快速重复音和左手大跨度的跳跃，在钢琴上创造出了同样的铃声效果，甚至更加华丽。这首作品是19世纪「超级改编」技法的最高成就之一。

这首作品实际上是李斯特一再修订的成果。他在1838至1839年间先完成了一个技术极为艰险的早期版本（称为「帕格尼尼练习曲」），1851年的定稿版在保持高难度的同时在音乐上更加精炼。此曲要求演奏者能在高音区以极弱的力度演奏极快速的重复音（模仿远处飘来的铃声），同时左手进行宽幅跳跃——这种技术要求在今天仍是衡量钢琴家技术的重要尺度之一。据说肖邦在听到帕格尼尼演奏原版小提琴曲时，曾感叹「如果这首曲子可以在钢琴上演奏就好了」，李斯特随即以这首改编版做出了回应。

《钟》是李斯特一系列帕格尼尼改编曲中最著名的一首，代表了浪漫主义时期「演奏家-作曲家」（performer-composer）以改编其他乐器或体裁的名曲来展示钢琴无限可能性这一传统的最高峰。它对斯克里亚宾、拉赫玛尼诺夫的炫技写作有直接影响，也激励了20世纪许多作曲家（如布拉姆斯、勃拉姆斯的《帕格尼尼变奏曲》）对帕格尼尼主题的重新创作与再诠释。
famousScores.detailEn:
La Campanella (No. 3 of the Grandes Études de Paganini, S. 141) was completed in 1851 as a piano transcription of the finale of Paganini's Violin Concerto No. 2 in B minor, Op. 7. The title 'La Campanella' ('The Little Bell') comes from the bell-like technique Paganini used in the original violin solo; Liszt transferred this effect to the piano with rapid repeated notes in the high register and wide left-hand leaps, creating the same bell-sound on the piano — if anything, even more brilliantly. It stands as one of the supreme achievements of the nineteenth-century 'super-transcription' tradition.

The piece is the product of multiple revisions. Liszt first completed an extremely demanding early version in 1838–39 (the 'Paganini Études'), and the 1851 final version is at once technically formidable and musically refined. It requires the performer to play extremely rapid repeated notes in the high register at a whisper-soft dynamic (evoking distant bells arriving on the wind) while the left hand makes sweeping leaps — a technical demand that remains a major benchmark for pianists today. Chopin, on hearing Paganini play the original violin version, is said to have exclaimed, 'How wonderful it would be if this could be done on the piano' — Liszt answered with this very transcription.

La Campanella is the most celebrated of Liszt's Paganini transcriptions and represents the apex of the Romantic 'performer-composer' tradition of demonstrating the piano's infinite possibilities by reimagining famous works from other instruments. Its influence on the virtuosic writing of Scriabin and Rachmaninoff was direct, and it inspired many twentieth-century composers — including Brahms (Paganini Variations) and Lutosławski — to create their own responses to Paganini's inexhaustible theme.
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Paganini Studies, S 141: Etude in G Sharp Minor - "La Campanella"

famousScores.name: 《苦路》 S.53
famousScores.desc: 李斯特晚期最深刻的宗教作品，极简和声预言了20世纪的音乐语言
famousScores.descEn: The most profound religious work of Liszt's late years — its radical harmonic austerity anticipates twentieth-century music
famousScores.detail:
《苦路》（Via Crucis，即「十字架之路」）创作于1878至1879年间，是李斯特晚期宗教作品中最具革命性的一部。全曲描绘基督被钉十字架前的14处苦路站（Stations of the Cross），为独唱、合唱和管风琴（或钢琴）而作。其和声语言极度压缩——空五度、平行声部、无解决的不协和音随处可见，整体音响极为枯寂，与同时代任何宗教音乐都截然不同。

这部作品在李斯特生前未能公演。他将总谱提交给几家出版社，均以「音乐太奇怪，无法出版」为由拒绝。《苦路》的世界首演发生于1929年，即李斯特去世43年后——此时德彪西已经改变了音乐的语言，勋伯格已走向了无调性。《苦路》的被遗忘与被重新发现的历史，是音乐史上最令人叹惋的故事之一。

20世纪音乐学界重新审视《苦路》后，普遍认为它是整个19世纪最超前的宗教音乐作品之一，其和声语言已将调性推至崩溃的边缘。梅西安（Messiaen）研读这部作品后深受震撼，认为它是自己创作的重要先驱之一。《苦路》是李斯特晚期「先知性」风格的最纯粹体现——一位活在19世纪的作曲家，写出了20世纪的音乐。
famousScores.detailEn:
Via Crucis ('The Way of the Cross') was composed in 1878–79 and is the most revolutionary of Liszt's late religious works. It portrays the fourteen Stations of the Cross for solo voices, chorus, and organ (or piano). Its harmonic language is radically compressed — bare fifths, parallel voice-leading, unresolved dissonances abound; the overall sound is gaunt and desolate, utterly unlike any contemporary religious music.

The work was not performed in Liszt's lifetime. He submitted the score to several publishers, all of whom rejected it as 'too strange to print.' The world premiere took place in 1929, forty-three years after Liszt's death — by which time Debussy had already transformed musical language and Schoenberg had moved into atonality. The history of Via Crucis's abandonment and eventual rediscovery is one of the most poignant in music history.

Twentieth-century musicology, upon re-examining the work, recognized it as one of the most prophetically advanced religious compositions of the entire nineteenth century, its harmonic language pushing tonality to the very edge of collapse. Messiaen, upon studying the score, was deeply moved and acknowledged it as one of his own important predecessors. Via Crucis is the purest expression of Liszt's late 'prophetic' style — a nineteenth-century composer who wrote music that belonged to the twentieth.

influence: 李斯特的影响广泛而深远。他的和声冒险——增三和弦的使用、全音阶、模糊调性——直接启发了瓦格纳的《特里斯坦和弦》和德彪西的印象主义。他发明的交响诗被理查·施特劳斯发扬光大。他的晚期作品的极端简约预示了梅西安和凯奇。作为教师，他免费教导了数百名学生，从中走出了整整一代钢琴学派的创始人。他发明了现代钢琴独奏会的模式。
influenceEn: Liszt's influence is vast and far-reaching. His harmonic daring — augmented triads, whole-tone scales, tonal ambiguity — directly inspired Wagner's Tristan chord and Debussy's Impressionism. The symphonic poem he invented was carried to new heights by Richard Strauss. The radical austerity of his late works anticipated Messiaen and Cage. As a teacher, he coached hundreds of students free of charge, producing an entire generation of founders of modern piano schools. He also invented the format of the modern solo recital.
